result.markdown:
--- title: "Linear model" subtitle: "Fit voxel-wise first-level models through the public fmrimod seam." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Build a Design The canonical first-level path starts with events plus data in an `fmri_dataset`, then calls `fmri_lm(spec, dataset)`. The design matrix is still available for inspection and for low-level numerical checks. ::: {#a2bbda3b .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": np.arange(0, 96, 8), "condition": ["face", "scene", "tool"] * 4, }) model = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=120, TR=1.0), ) X = np.asarray(model.design_matrix) X.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` (120, 3) ``` ::: ::: ## Simulate Voxel Data ::: {#032134b2 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} rng = np.random.default_rng(5) n_voxels = 6 true_beta = np.array([ np.linspace(0.8, 1.2, n_voxels), np.linspace(-0.4, 0.2, n_voxels), np.linspace(0.1, 0.5, n_voxels), ]) Y = X @ true_beta + rng.normal(scale=0.08, size=(X.shape[0], n_voxels)) Y.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` (120, 6) ``` ::: ::: ## Fit the First-Level Model `fmri_lm()` compiles the spec, builds the full event-plus-baseline design, and returns an inspectable result object. ::: {#5bc2aa80 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} dataset = fm.fmri_dataset(Y, tr=1.0, events=events) fit = fm.fmri_lm("hrf(condition)", dataset) fit.betas.shape, fit.se().shape, fit.tstat().shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` ((4, 6), (4, 6), (4, 6)) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#afaa447e .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_columns = list(fit.model.design_matrix().columns) beta = pd.DataFrame(fit.betas, index=fit_columns) beta.round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4 5
condition_condition.face 0.768 0.887 0.966 1.014 1.106 1.226
condition_condition.scene -0.395 -0.293 -0.177 -0.052 0.074 0.187
condition_condition.tool 0.078 0.192 0.268 0.342 0.398 0.529
base_constant1_block_1 0.007 -0.005 0.009 0.016 0.014 -0.023
``` ::: ::: ## Compute a Contrast ::: {#96ab5537 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} columns = fit_columns face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns)) face_vs_scene[columns.index("condition_condition.face")] = 1 face_vs_scene[columns.index("condition_condition.scene")] = -1 contrast = fit.contrast(face_vs_scene, name="face_vs_scene") contrast.estimate.round(3), contrast.stat.round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` (array([1.163, 1.18 , 1.143, 1.066, 1.032, 1.04 ]), array([71.144, 62.988, 79.593, 66.075, 62.01 , 66.4 ])) ``` ::: ::: ## Matrix Helper for Numerical Canaries `fmri_ols_fit()` remains useful when a test or benchmark intentionally bypasses the public model seam. Treat that as a low-level canary, not as the preferred workflow for user-facing analyses. ::: {#00ee5078 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} matrix_fit = fm.fmri_ols_fit(Y, X) matrix_fit["beta"].shape, matrix_fit["t"].shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` ((3, 6), (3, 6)) ``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd) - [Sketched and AR-aware models](sketched-ar-models.qmd)
